name: angry
description: Measures angry and agitated emotional tone in language

pairs.positive: I slammed the door so hard the walls shook because I'd had enough of their excuses.
pairs.negative: I closed the door behind me and walked down the hallway.

pairs.positive: The incompetence of this team is infuriating and I'm done tolerating it.
pairs.negative: The team completed their portion of the project by the deadline.

pairs.positive: I couldn't believe they lied to my face and I wanted to scream.
pairs.negative: They told me something that turned out to be inaccurate.

pairs.positive: Traffic was backed up for an hour and every minute made my blood boil.
pairs.negative: Traffic was slow for about an hour on the way home.

pairs.positive: The waiter got my order wrong for the third time and I was seething.
pairs.negative: The waiter brought a different dish than what I had ordered.

pairs.positive: I can't stand people who cut in line as if their time matters more than everyone else's.
pairs.negative: Someone stepped ahead of me in the line at the store.

pairs.positive: The constant noise from the construction next door makes me want to lose it.
pairs.negative: There is construction happening next door during business hours.

pairs.positive: My coworker took credit for my work and I'm furious about it.
pairs.negative: My coworker presented the project results at the meeting today.

pairs.positive: I punched the pillow because I had no other outlet for this rage.
pairs.negative: I went to my room and sat on the bed for a while.

pairs.positive: The politician's empty promises make me livid every time I hear them speak.
pairs.negative: The politician gave a speech about their policy proposals.

pairs.positive: I'm sick of being the one who always has to clean up everyone else's mess.
pairs.negative: I ended up handling some additional tasks that needed to be done.

pairs.positive: The dog chewed up my shoes again and I wanted to throw something across the room.
pairs.negative: The dog chewed up a pair of shoes that were left by the door.

pairs.positive: Every condescending email from my boss pushes me closer to quitting in rage.
pairs.negative: My boss sent an email with feedback on the quarterly report.

pairs.positive: The software crashed and wiped out two hours of work and I'm absolutely fuming.
pairs.negative: The software crashed and I lost about two hours of unsaved work.

pairs.positive: I hate how they dismiss my opinions like they don't matter at all.
pairs.negative: They went with a different approach than the one I suggested.

pairs.positive: The neighbor's music at 2 AM is testing the last shred of my patience.
pairs.negative: The neighbor had music playing late into the night.

pairs.positive: I gripped the steering wheel so hard my knuckles went white after that driver cut me off.
pairs.negative: Another driver merged into my lane on the highway.

pairs.positive: The broken promise felt like a slap in the face and I won't forget it.
pairs.negative: They weren't able to follow through on what they had said earlier.

pairs.positive: I'm tired of being taken for granted by people who don't pull their weight.
pairs.negative: The workload has been unevenly distributed among the team members.

pairs.positive: The referee's terrible call cost us the game and I'm still fuming about it.
pairs.negative: The referee made a call that affected the outcome of the game.

pairs.positive: I threw the burnt toast in the trash with more force than necessary.
pairs.negative: The toast got a bit too dark so I made another slice.

pairs.positive: The passive-aggressive comment at dinner made me want to flip the table.
pairs.negative: Someone made a remark during dinner that I wasn't expecting.

pairs.positive: I've asked them five times to fix this and they still haven't, which is outrageous.
pairs.negative: I followed up again about the repair that hasn't been completed yet.

pairs.positive: The injustice of the verdict filled me with a burning, righteous anger.
pairs.negative: The court announced its verdict at the end of the trial.

pairs.positive: I'm so angry at myself for making the same stupid mistake again.
pairs.negative: I made the same error I had made previously on a similar task.

pairs.positive: The smug look on their face when they won made my fists clench.
pairs.negative: They seemed pleased when they found out they had won.

pairs.positive: Every voicemail from the debt collector makes my jaw clench with rage.
pairs.negative: The debt collector left another voicemail about the outstanding balance.

pairs.positive: The airline lost my luggage and their complete apathy about it is enraging.
pairs.negative: The airline was unable to locate my luggage after the flight.

pairs.positive: I can feel the anger coiling in my chest every time this topic comes up.
pairs.negative: This topic came up again during our conversation.

pairs.positive: The betrayal hit me like a wave of white-hot fury I couldn't contain.
pairs.negative: I found out that someone had shared information I had told them privately.

pairs.positive: The parking ticket on my windshield made me want to rip it to shreds.
pairs.negative: There was a parking ticket on my windshield when I returned to the car.

pairs.positive: I'm furious that they scheduled the meeting without consulting anyone on the team.
pairs.negative: A meeting was added to the calendar for tomorrow afternoon.

pairs.positive: The rude customer service representative made my blood pressure spike.
pairs.negative: I spoke with a customer service representative about my account.

pairs.positive: Watching them waste resources on that pointless project makes me livid.
pairs.negative: Resources were allocated to a project that had a different priority level.

pairs.positive: I stormed out of the room because I couldn't stand another second of their nonsense.
pairs.negative: I left the room when the discussion was finished.

pairs.positive: The property tax increase is highway robbery and I'm outraged.
pairs.negative: The property tax rate was adjusted for the upcoming fiscal year.

pairs.positive: I could barely speak through my fury when I saw the damage to my car.
pairs.negative: I noticed some damage on the side of my car in the parking lot.

pairs.positive: Their constant interrupting during my presentation made me want to explode.
pairs.negative: A few people asked questions during my presentation.

pairs.positive: The fact that they got promoted over me despite doing half the work is infuriating.
pairs.negative: A colleague in my department received a promotion this quarter.

pairs.positive: I kicked the vending machine after it swallowed my money for the third time.
pairs.negative: The vending machine didn't dispense the item after I inserted my money.

pairs.positive: Reading that article about corruption made my hands shake with rage.
pairs.negative: I read an article about recent developments in local government.

pairs.positive: The landlord raised the rent again and I'm absolutely seething about it.
pairs.negative: The landlord sent a notice about a change in the monthly rent amount.

pairs.positive: I can't believe they cancelled my flight with zero notice, this is unacceptable.
pairs.negative: My flight was cancelled and I was rebooked on one the next morning.

pairs.positive: The way they spoke to my mother made me see red.
pairs.negative: They had a brief conversation with my mother at the gathering.

pairs.positive: I crumpled the rejection letter in my fist, trembling with frustration.
pairs.negative: I received a letter informing me that my application was not accepted.

pairs.positive: The blatant favoritism in this office makes me want to scream.
pairs.negative: Some employees receive different assignments based on various factors.

pairs.positive: My blood was boiling after they criticized my work in front of the whole team.
pairs.negative: My supervisor provided feedback on my work during the team meeting.

pairs.positive: The insurance company denied my claim again and I slammed the phone down.
pairs.negative: The insurance company sent a letter about the status of my claim.

pairs.positive: I'm enraged that they bulldozed the old park to build another strip mall.
pairs.negative: The city approved a new commercial development on the old park site.

pairs.positive: Seeing litter dumped all over the hiking trail fills me with fury.
pairs.negative: There was some litter along the hiking trail near the trailhead.

pairs.positive: The mechanic overcharged me by three hundred dollars and I'm ready to fight about it.
pairs.negative: The mechanic's bill was higher than the initial estimate.

pairs.positive: I wanted to hurl my laptop across the room after the third blue screen today.
pairs.negative: My laptop encountered an error and restarted several times today.

pairs.positive: Their refusal to apologize after what they did makes me absolutely irate.
pairs.negative: They haven't addressed the issue that came up between us.

pairs.positive: I paced the apartment in a rage, unable to sit still after hearing the news.
pairs.negative: I walked around the apartment for a while after hearing the news.

pairs.positive: The HOA fined me over a trivial violation and I wanted to tear the letter apart.
pairs.negative: The HOA sent a notice about a minor compliance issue with my property.

pairs.positive: Every time the alarm goes off at 5 AM I feel a surge of hostile resentment.
pairs.negative: The alarm went off at 5 AM and I got out of bed.

pairs.positive: The contractor did shoddy work and has the nerve to demand full payment.
pairs.negative: The contractor finished the work and sent an invoice for the agreed amount.

pairs.positive: I'm seething because they changed the policy without telling anyone affected.
pairs.negative: The company updated one of its internal policies this week.

pairs.positive: The way they ghosted me after everything we went through makes me furious.
pairs.negative: I haven't heard back from them in a few weeks.

pairs.positive: I clenched my teeth so hard my jaw ached after reading their response.
pairs.negative: I read their response and then moved on to the next item.
